_meta.description: Built-in catalog of BTP risk rules. Loaded by ConflictSetSeeder on startup when conflict_sets is empty. Admins can edit / disable / delete after loading; the seeder will not overwrite once any rule exists. Update this file + bump the version + clear the table to re-seed on dev environments.

rules.name: Global Account Administrator
rules.description: Holds full control over the global account - can create / destroy subaccounts, assign any role collection.

rules.name: Subaccount Administrator
rules.description: Full control over a subaccount - role collections, destinations, services. One holder is one too many; this is a break-glass-only role.

rules.name: User and Role Administrator
rules.description: Can grant any role collection to any user. Indirect path to every other privilege in the subaccount.

rules.name: Destination Administrator
rules.description: Can edit destinations including their stored credentials - directly exfil-able by anyone holding this role.

rules.name: Cloud Connector Administrator
rules.description: Can configure on-prem connectivity - backdoor into the customer's corporate network.

rules.name: Audit Log Administrator
rules.description: Can modify audit-log configuration. Combined with Viewer permits self-erasure of incident evidence.

rules.name: Connectivity and Destination Administrator
rules.description: Full destination + connectivity admin in one role - same exfil concern as Destination Admin, plus on-prem reach.

rules.name: Audit log conflict (Admin + Viewer)
rules.description: Holding both Audit Log Administrator and Audit Log Viewer lets one user inspect AND tamper with the audit trail - bypasses the four-eyes principle on evidence preservation.

rules.name: Privilege escalation pair (Subaccount Admin + User Admin)
rules.description: Either alone is critical; both together creates a closed loop where the user can grant themselves any future privilege without external review.

rules.name: Destination + Connectivity admin pair
rules.description: Holding both Destination Administrator and Cloud Connector Administrator allows a single user to exfiltrate corporate-network credentials AND route external traffic into the corporate network.

rules.name: Over-privileged user (10+ role collections)
rules.description: User holds 10 or more role collections in the subaccount. Empirically the inflection point above which separation of duties almost certainly fails; merits a review even if no specific conflict triggers.

rules.name: External email account
rules.description: User's email domain is not on the internal allow-list - usually an external partner or a stale guest invitation. Set the internal domains globally via the btpc.external-email.internal-domains property, or per-rule by editing this row and listing domains in the 'role collections' field (one per line).
